trevor-bachmeyer.acute_vs_chronic_note: Dr Bachmeyer often distinguishes acute (injury/infection) vs chronic (longevity/maintenance) dosing. Acute_signals + chronic_signals fields surface candidate contexts; full distillation pending Codex pass.

reset_biology_synthesis.recommendation: Reset Biology suggests asking the practitioner to consider DHEA as an oral neurosteroid adjunct to TRT — not a stand-alone — using Hunter's most recent dose framing as the operative range: Hunter (E7GPNidicqY 01:21:33.000): "if you're going to do both, would be 25 to 50 mg of DHEA in the morning and then 10 to 25 mg of pregnenolone at night." We'd ask the prescribing physician to weigh whether the patient is one of the men or women who actually need it, since Hunter (E7GPNidicqY 01:21:41.240) immediately hedges "Uh but you don't always need that. Some men don't need that. I don't take DHEA," and Taylor (hgI5Mpc-xDQ 01:57:53.199) flags an anxiety contraindication: "if you can't take pregnn alolone and DHEA because the anxiety." In women, Taylor (JccJTHbWF84 01:03:21.200) suggests a clearly different role — "the need for vaginal estradile and DHEA can help. And then DHEA aromatizes as well" — so the practitioner should pick route (oral vs vaginal) by patient context.
reset_biology_synthesis.caveats:
- Hunter does not personally take it: Hunter (E7GPNidicqY 01:21:41.240): "Uh but you don't always need that. Some men don't need that. I don't take DHEA, although I did get some injectable DHEA that I'm going to experiment with."
- Anxiety contraindication (Taylor's clinical population): Taylor (hgI5Mpc-xDQ 01:57:53.199): "that's a tough one if you can't take pregnn alolone and DHEA because the anxiety."
- Aromatizes to estradiol — surface for estrogen-sensitive patients: Taylor (JccJTHbWF84 01:03:23.280): "DHEA aromatizes as well."
- Trevor frames DHEA primarily as a LAB MARKER for cortisol/adrenal function, not a daily supplement: Trevor (k43GkcxIgjc 00:23:33.200): "When cortisol is high, which means you have low DHEA, panic, fatigue, an aggressive massive overreaction."
- Trevor is explicitly NEGATIVE on DHEA for dementia/cognition: Trevor (UrlUjuiur-8 00:15:04.160): "Take this DHEA, take these omega-3s, right? Take Aerisep, take L-dopa. None of those have worked in the history. Dementia and Alzheimer's medication have a 99.6% failure rate."
- Pregnenolone-as-precursor framing — DHEA sits downstream: Hunter email threadId 19a2b80bfe4b9856 (2025-10-28): "It sits at the top of the steroid hormone cascade and serves as the starting point for progesterone, DHEA, cortisol, aldosterone, and eventually testosterone and estradiol"; Hunter same email (2025-10-28): "low or low-normal pregnenolone and often low DHEA-S and modestly low male progesterone" identify good candidates for adjunctive support.
- Postmenopausal physiology context: Trevor (hbo13qmxx-k 00:17:25.039): "the hormonal environment for your adrenal glands and any remaining or ovarian stromal tissue to produce what little testosterone and DHEA they still can."
reset_biology_synthesis.cross_expert_consensus: All three experts treat DHEA as a steroid-cascade signal worth tracking, not a peptide in the BPC/TB-500 sense. Hunter (E7GPNidicqY 01:21:26.120): "In addition to TRT, suggested addition dosing of oral DHEA and pregnenolone for men, morning or night." Taylor (Cg7JtpnfRY4 01:21:26.120, same co-hosted episode): "In addition to TRT, suggested addition dosing of oral DHEA and pregnenolone for men morning or night." Trevor (1r5FQHIx5-o 00:52:53.040): "if you were going to track any metrics in your experiment because people are going to ask, I would look at CRP, homoyine, body comp for sure, right? sleep quality, libido, mental clarity, IGF-1, DHEA. That's about it." Hunter and Taylor also agree on the adjunct-to-TRT framing — both deliver the 25–50 mg morning dosing line in the 4/18/26 Saturday Morning Coffee Talk co-hosted by them (videoIds E7GPNidicqY and Cg7JtpnfRY4 are the same show on each partner's channel).
reset_biology_synthesis.cross_expert_disagreement: Whether DHEA is a clinically useful adjunct vs largely unnecessary. Hunter (E7GPNidicqY 01:21:33.000): "would be 25 to 50 mg of DHEA in the morning," but immediately hedges Hunter (E7GPNidicqY 01:21:41.240): "you don't always need that. Some men don't need that. I don't take DHEA." Taylor (JccJTHbWF84 01:03:21.200): "the need for vaginal estradile and DHEA can help" — i.e., useful, but in a different (vaginal-route, female) clinical context, NOT the oral 25-50 mg male protocol. Trevor never gives a DHEA dose at all — he uses DHEA only as a downstream marker (Trevor k43GkcxIgjc 00:23:33.200: "When cortisol is high, which means you have low DHEA") and is openly negative on supplementing it for cognition: Trevor (UrlUjuiur-8 00:15:04.160): "Take this DHEA, take these omega-3s, right? Take Aerisep, take L-dopa. None of those have worked." Route also differs: Hunter (E7GPNidicqY 01:21:43.920) says "I did get some injectable DHEA that I'm going to experiment with" — explicitly experimental, not standard — while Hunter's primary recommendation is oral, Taylor's female recommendation is vaginal, and Trevor offers no route at all.

chronic.dose: Hunter (E7GPNidicqY 01:21:33.000): "if you're going to do both, would be 25 to 50 mg of DHEA in the morning and then 10 to 25 mg of pregnenolone at night."
chronic.route: Hunter (E7GPNidicqY 01:21:26.120): "oral DHEA and pregnenolone for men, morning or night." Hunter (E7GPNidicqY 01:21:43.920) experimental note: "I did get some injectable DHEA that I'm going to experiment with." Taylor (JccJTHbWF84 01:03:21.200) female route: "the need for vaginal estradile and DHEA can help."
chronic.frequency: Hunter (E7GPNidicqY 01:21:28.200): "morning or night. My preference, if you're going to do both, would be 25 to 50 mg of DHEA in the morning."
chronic.verbatim_citation: Hunter (E7GPNidicqY 01:21:33.000): "if you're going to do both, would be 25 to 50 mg of DHEA in the morning and then 10 to 25 mg of pregnenolone at night." Hedge: Hunter (E7GPNidicqY 01:21:41.240): "you don't always need that. Some men don't need that. I don't take DHEA."

reset_biology_synthesis.female_specific_notes: Taylor (JccJTHbWF84 01:03:21.200): "the need for vaginal estradile and DHEA can help. And then DHEA aromatizes as well" — vaginal route + estrogen-aromatization context for women on HRT. Taylor (hgI5Mpc-xDQ 01:57:53.199): "that's a tough one if you can't take pregnn alolone and DHEA because the anxiety" — anxiety as a real contraindication in women on HCG/HRT stacks. Trevor (hbo13qmxx-k 00:17:25.039) frames postmenopausal physiology: "the hormonal environment for your adrenal glands and any remaining or ovarian stromal tissue to produce what little testosterone and DHEA they still can" — DHEA endogenous production drops, supporting the case for adjunctive replacement in postmenopausal women on HRT. Reset Biology would ask the prescribing physician whether oral 25-50 mg (Hunter's male protocol) or vaginal application (Taylor's female framing) is appropriate to the patient's symptoms, labs, and TRT/HRT base.
